notes.implement: Updated V3.1 addressing language-consistency issues; supports thinking/non-thinking modes; 164K ctx. Aigon-curated default as of 2026-04-29 — passed brewboard bench in 3m51s and was the fastest probe across the V3.x family. Promoted from a candidate slot after the previous default (deepseek-chat-v3.1) timed out at 10m on the same bench task today.

summary.headline: Best default for cheap OpenRouter implement loops; bench-backed but aging.
summary.body: Reliability patch over V3.1 fixing language mixing and agent templates. Aigon brewboard PASS Apr 2026 (3m51s). Published SWE-bench multilingual 57.8%. Pricing drifted up to $0.27/$0.95 MTok.

notes.implement: Original V3.1; was the curated default until 2026-04-29 when it failed the brewboard bench (10-minute timeout) despite passing single-turn probes. Kept active for backward-compatible benchmark continuity; pick deepseek-v3.1-terminus instead for new work.

Newest DeepSeek (Apr 2026); 1M context, 13B active params; cost-efficient flash variant. Probe-passed (warm: ~22s) but bench timed out at 10m on 2026-04-29 — flash-tier cannot sustain multi-turn agentic loops.

quarantined.reason: 0/1 on brewboard bench (10m timeout 2026-04-29). Flash-tier active-param count too small for sustained agentic tool-use loops; superseded by deepseek-v4-pro for agentic work.

notes.implement: Bench-passed in 3m27s on 2026-04-29. QUARANTINED 2026-05-11: triggered opencode compaction infinite loop bug (issue #22329 — open, unresolved). Thinking mode causes reasoning_content to be dropped by opencode on turn 2+, generating 400 errors and retries; combined with auto-compaction loop, produces 50-100+ tool_call turns on simple tasks. Confirmed: burned ~$20 of OpenRouter credits in under an hour on a footer-add benchmark. Do not run until opencode issue #22329 is resolved and tested.

quarantined.reason: Triggers opencode compaction infinite loop (issue #22329, open). DeepSeek thinking mode + opencode auto-compaction = unbounded tool_call loop. A simple 3-turn footer task ran 50-100+ turns and burned ~$20 of OpenRouter credits. finish_reason never reaches 'stop'. Do not run until opencode #22329 is confirmed fixed.
quarantined.evidence: OpenRouter logs 2026-05-11T07:27 AEST: 15+ visible rows all finish_reason=tool_calls, input growing 7K→27K per call, multiple providers (NovitaAI/SiliconFlow/Parasail). GitHub: sst/opencode #22329 (DeepSeek compaction loop), #25000 (reasoning_content dropped).

notes.implement: Bench-passed in 6m52s on 2026-04-29. QUARANTINED 2026-05-11: same opencode compaction loop as V4 Pro (issue #22329 affects all DeepSeek models). Also reported in RooCode #9779 (Dec 2025): empty assistant responses and tool-call loops in opencode. Inherently slow (35 tok/s median). Superseded by V3.1 Terminus which passed the bench and is not affected by the compaction loop.

quarantined.reason: All DeepSeek models affected by opencode compaction infinite loop (issue #22329). Also has tool-call loop failures in opencode reported Dec 2025 (RooCode #9779). Slow by nature (35 tok/s median). Superseded by deepseek-v3.1-terminus which is unaffected.
quarantined.evidence: opencode GitHub issue #22329 (all DeepSeek models); RooCode #9779 Dec 2025; OpenRouter logs 2026-05-11 showing same finish_reason:tool_calls loop pattern.

notes.implement: Ultra-cheap general-purpose. Capable for routine implementation; weaker on complex reasoning.

archived.reason: ID does not exist in opencode's internal model registry. Only the :free variant (openrouter/meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free) is registered, which is rate-limited and unsuitable for agentic work. No paid equivalent routable via opencode.
archived.evidence: aigon agent-probe op --model returned empty response in 961ms; research confirmed ProviderModelNotFoundError fires locally before any API call.

notes.implement: Code-optimised, low latency; strong on agentic loops and tool use.

quarantined.reason: Probe timed out at 45s on a trivial PONG prompt; bench sweeps consistently hang past the 10-minute per-pair ceiling. OpenRouter routing for this model appears unhealthy or the upstream is consistently slow — distinct from the quota/credit issues that affected the other op models on the same date.
quarantined.evidence: aigon agent-probe op --all on 2026-04-29 returned FAIL TIMEOUT (>45s) for grok-code-fast-1 while every other op model PASSed in 2.7-5.3s on the same run. Prior bench sweeps on 2026-04-28 also showed grok-code-fast-1 at 461s (vs ~30-60s for healthy agents).

notes.implement: Alibaba flagship MoE; 262K ctx; excellent cost/perf at sub-cent input. Canonical OpenCode/OpenRouter id is `-2507`; legacy `-07-25` aliases normalize at launch. Tools-capable on OpenRouter. 11 providers, 7.8x speed spread.

summary.headline: Excellent value for implementation loops; not suitable for spec or research work.
summary.body: Sub-cent input pricing with strong implement scores on Aigon bench. MoE flagship via OpenRouter with 262K context. Lacks curated notes for spec/research roles — use a dedicated spec/research model instead.

notes.implement: Code-specialist MoE; 156K ctx; quarantined — consistently times out on simple prompts.

quarantined.reason: Consistently times out (>45s) on a trivial one-shot PONG probe via opencode run. Likely no healthy endpoints on OpenRouter at time of testing.
quarantined.evidence: aigon agent-probe op --model returned TIMEOUT on two consecutive runs (2026-04-28).

notes.implement: Reasoning variant; thinking mode causes token runaway on multi-turn agentic loops — consistently times out.

quarantined.reason: 0/3 on brewboard bench (2 timeout, 1 error). Thinking mode triggers unbounded token spend on multi-turn tool-use loops; never signals completion within 10m ceiling.

notes.implement: Mistral's coding-agent specialist (July 2025 build); tuned for SWE-bench style tool use; 128K ctx. Superseded by devstral-2512 for new work but kept active for backward-compat benchmark continuity.

quarantined.reason: Removed from OpenRouter catalog (STALE-ID). OpenCode cannot route this model id.
quarantined.evidence: openrouter.ai/api/v1/models — no row for mistralai/devstral-small-2507 as of 2026-07-11; superseded by devstral-2512.

notes.implement: Newest Devstral (Dec 2025); state-of-the-art open-source agentic coding; 256K ctx. Probed PASS in 4.1s. More expensive than devstral-small-2507 but a genuine generational jump for agentic coding.
Alibaba flagship purpose-built coder; 480B MoE (35B active params); probed PASS in 5.4s. Distinct from the quarantined qwen3-coder-30b-a3b; this is the full-size flagship variant at very low cost.

summary.headline: Top open MoE coder for agentic loops; bench before promoting to defaults.
summary.body: 480B MoE with 35B active params; Qwen team claims Sonnet-4-class agentic coding. Independent SWE-bench Verified ~68%. OpenRouter at $0.22/$1.80 MTok. Probe PASS 4.3s Jul 2026.

notes.implement: Mistral code-specialist (Aug 2025 build); tuned for code completion and generation; probed PASS in 3.1s. Cheapest Mistral agentic-coding option.

summary.headline: Cheapest Mistral agent; probe-only — unverified on multi-turn bench.
summary.body: Mistral code-specialist Aug 2025 build. Probe PASS 4.3s. No Aigon bench or external SWE score curated yet. Use for cost-sensitive completion, not default implement.

notes.implement: Mid-tier Devstral (Jul 2025); fills the gap between devstral-small and devstral-2512; probed PASS in 5.3s. Note: openrouter/mistralai/devstral-medium fails (wrong ID) — use this 2507-suffixed form.

quarantined.reason: Removed from OpenRouter catalog (STALE-ID). OpenCode cannot route this model id.
quarantined.evidence: openrouter.ai/api/v1/models — no row for mistralai/devstral-medium-2507 as of 2026-07-11; use devstral-2512 instead.

quarantined.evidence: openrouter.ai/api/v1/models — no row for x-ai/grok-4-fast as of 2026-07-11.

notes.implement: Z.ai flagship reasoning model (Jun 2026); 1M ctx; OpenRouter reports tools support. Probed PASS in 8.4s on 2026-06-26. Successor to quarantined glm-5.1 — score pending brewboard bench.

summary.headline: Long-context open coder; pricing dropped sharply — bench before trust.
summary.body: 1M ctx MIT-licensed MoE; vendor Terminal-Bench 81.0 / SWE-bench Pro 62.1. OpenRouter now $0.35/$1.10 MTok (down from registry $0.95/$3.00). Probe PASS 5.1s. Prior glm-5.1 quarantined for bench hangs — do not inherit score.

notes.implement: Capable reasoning model; quarantined — OpenRouter routing reports no tool-use endpoints (2026-04-27).

quarantined.reason: OpenRouter /api/v1/models reports model exists but supported_parameters does not include 'tools'. OpenCode tool-use sessions return 'No endpoints found that support tool use'.
